title: Unified query-time hybrid retriever: fuse dense cosine + BM25 over L3+L2
source: Ash memory-retrieval review 2026-07-12 (hackathon Track 1 — this is the flagship retrieval feature)
current_state: Three uncoordinated retrieval paths: (a) get_l3_context() injects an importance-ranked dump with query='' (NOT query-relevant); (b) _hybrid_search_l3() is BM25 lexical, L3 only; (c) memory_search_semantic() is dense cosine but L2 only and only runs when the LLM explicitly calls the tool. There is no single query -> ranked-results function, and dense retrieval never covers L3. Result: paraphrased recall misses.
target_state: One method MemoryTools.retrieve(query, k=6, tiers=('l3','l2')) -> List[dict] that: embeds the query once; scores candidate rows by a fused signal = normalized-BM25 + dense-cosine + the existing importance/entity nudges; returns the top-k canonical dicts each with a 'score' and 'tier'. Degrades to BM25-only when no embedding provider is configured. _hybrid_search_l3 and memory_search_semantic become thin back-compat wrappers over retrieve().
migration_plan: 1. Implement retrieve(): embed query via memory.semantic_dedup.get_embedding; pull active L3 rows (with embeddings from T-291) and L2 rows (embeddings in metadata); compute per-signal scores.

2. Fuse: min-max normalize BM25 and cosine independently to [0,1], then weighted sum (defaults e.g. 0.5 dense + 0.4 bm25 + 0.1 importance), weights overridable via env (PI_RETRIEVE_W_DENSE etc). Keep the existing BM25_FLOOR / entity bonus logic reachable.
3. Return top-k dedup'd canonical dicts (reuse the {id,content,importance,category,tier,score} shape).
4. Rewrite _hybrid_search_l3 and memory_search_semantic as wrappers so existing callers/tests keep working.

5. Reproducing unit test (the proof): plant an L3 fact 'the lab uses ZEBRAFISH as the model organism'; query 'what model organism do we use' (no lexical overlap with 'zebrafish'); assert retrieve() returns it in the top-k, AND assert BM25-alone (_hybrid_search_l3) does NOT — demonstrating the gap this closes.
6. Run the existing BM25/L3 tests + python scripts/verify.py — all PASS.
risk_notes: Score-fusion tuning is the main risk — keep weights defaulted and env-overridable, don't hand-tune to one test. Must NOT regress existing lexical behavior: run test_l3_search_fast_path.py and test_memory_tier_contract.py. If no embedding provider is configured the method must return exactly the old BM25 result (pure additive). Keep retrieve() self-contained in tools_memory.py; do not introduce a vector-DB dependency — SQLite + in-process cosine is sufficient at this scale (< a few thousand rows).
